WATERLOO, Iowa The VGM Group has pulled together suggested talking points for providers who plan comment CMS’s proposal include vents its next round competitive bidding.
“There are major concerns with life-sustaining equipment such ventilators being restricted contracted suppliers given the instability that the program has caused within the DMEPOS industry,” VGM stated bulletin.
CMS has proposed adding the following codes the program: E<hide> (invasive), E<hide> (non-invasive) and E<hide> (multi-functioning).
The agency expects roll out the next round the program <hide>, after two-year gap period.
VGM encourages providers use real examples their comments.
“Providing real examples patients who suppliers serve can effectively demonstrate the need keep this product category out the competitive bidding program,” VGM stated.
This market serves highly vulnerable patient population with life-sustaining equipment.
Vent patients require highly skilled respiratory therapists beyond the average oxygen patient.
Ventilation small market with few suppliers certain areas having proper resources.
The negative impacts from competitive bidding the DME community should not replicated life-sustaining equipment such vents.
CMS will accept comments its proposal until Dec.
Send comments DMEPOS@cms.hhs.gov.
